turns.content: OK OK OK I literally cannot contain myself right now I have the BIGGEST news!!!
Alright I can feel the energy through the screen! What's going on??
so you know how I've been grinding, booking my own gigs, building the brand and all that?? WELL. I just signed a residency at REBEL!!!
Wait, Rebel as in the massive venue on the waterfront?? That's a huge deal! How did this come together?

YES THAT REBEL!! the one on Polson Pier!! ok so basically the promoter who books their Saturday nights heard one of my sets at another spot a few weeks back and reached out. we had a meeting, I played him some mixes, and he offered me the Saturday night slot!! EVERY Saturday!!
Every Saturday night at Rebel is a serious commitment and a serious platform. That venue draws huge crowds. What kind of deal did they put together for you?

fam they're paying me $1,100 per set!! that's almost DOUBLE what I was getting at the Drake!! like the Drake was cute and it served its purpose but $1,100 a NIGHT?? every week?? do the math on that ahlie

That's roughly $4,400 a month just from the residency alone — before any other gigs or production work. That's a significant and stable income stream. Going from a smaller venue to Rebel is a real level up in both pay and visibility. Are you handling the booking yourself since you're self-managing?

YEP!! negotiated every detail myself!! no manager taking a cut, no middleman. every dollar of that $1,100 is MINE. and honestly that's the part that makes me the proudest still. I did this on my OWN merit

That's a testament to the work you've been putting in. Negotiating your own deals and landing a venue like Rebel is no small thing. What about the technical side — Rebel's a big room. Are you adjusting your sets for a larger space?

oh my GOD don't even get me started on the sound system!! they have 28,000 watts in there!! TWENTY EIGHT THOUSAND. like when I went for the walkthrough and they let me test the system I nearly CRIED. my riddims on that kind of power?? the bass was shaking my BONES fam. I've never felt my own music like that before

28,000 watts is arena-level sound. That's a completely different experience from a club-sized system. Your dancehall and bass-heavy production style is going to absolutely thrive with that kind of power behind it. Just keep in mind — at that wattage, every detail in your mix gets magnified. Any muddiness in the low end will be ten times more obvious. Are you going to adjust your production or your set preparation for that?

100%!! I already started reworking some of my tracks specifically for that system. tightening up the sub bass, making sure the kicks hit clean. I went in the lab last night and A/B'd everything on my monitors imagining it through 28K watts lol. it's a whole different game but THIS is the game I want to be in!!

Smart approach — tailoring your tracks for the system they'll actually be played on. A lot of DJs don't think about that. You might also want to go in early before a set and do a sound check with some of your reworked tracks, get familiar with how the room responds. Every big venue has its own acoustic character. Where in the frequency range does the room build up? Where does it fall off? Knowing that will let you EQ on the fly during sets.

that's EXACTLY the plan!! the sound tech there already said I can come in early on Saturdays to check levels. this is the level up I've been WAITING for still. like everything I've been doing — the grinding, dropping the manager, building the brand — it was all leading to THIS. the riddim is finally right ahlie!!!

You've earned this. The self-management, the relentless gigging, the constant production work — it all built the reputation that got you in that room. Rebel is going to put you in front of a much bigger audience every week, and with a sound system like that, people are going to FEEL your music the way it was meant to be felt. Enjoy the first Saturday — it's going to be special.
